methodology: Smaller models: Mac Studio M4 Max / M3 Ultra tok/s are aligned to published MLX Q4 bands where available; Mac mini tiers are scaled heuristically. Large / frontier models (200B+, MoE, Kimi-class) use conservative placeholder tok/s and RAM gates — validate on your exact quant, context, and runtime; many are run sharded across multiple Macs in practice.

sources.title: InsiderLLM — M4 Max and M3 Ultra for Local LLMs (MLX Q4 tables)
sources.note: Llama 8B, Qwen3 14B/32B, Llama 3.3 70B tok/s bands on Mac Studio; M3 Ultra vs M4 Max bandwidth discussion

sources.note: Why advertised tok/s can differ from sustained interactive use

sources.note: Additional MLX vs GGUF samples on Apple hardware

sources.title: Virge.io — exo distributed inference on Mac clusters
sources.note: Large models (e.g. DeepSeek V3, Qwen3-235B, Kimi) on multi-Mac setups — aggregate tok/s examples

sources.title: Chatbot Arena (LMSys) — community Elo leaderboard
sources.note: Used as primary source for arena sub-score in usefulness composite

sources.title: HumanEval / LiveCodeBench — coding benchmark aggregates
sources.note: Coding sub-score derived from relative ranking on multi-round coding evals

sources.title: Hacker News / oMLX — Qwen3-30B-A3B on M4 Max (~70-100 tok/s) and M4 Pro 16c (~89 tok/s TG)
sources.note: Qwen3 MoE throughput on Apple Silicon; MoE active-param efficiency means high tok/s despite large total params

sources.title: Medium / Rif Kiamil — Gemma 3 27B on Mac Studio M3 Ultra (33 tok/s LM Studio MLX)
sources.note: Gemma 3 27B tok/s on M3 Ultra and M4 Max; MLX vs Ollama comparison

sources.note: Llama 4 Scout 109B MoE Q4 requires ~61GB unified RAM; active params only 17B

sources.note: MLX 4-bit quantized GLM-5 model card; lists ~419GB Apple Silicon memory footprint

sources.title: mlx-community — GLM-5-MXFP4-Q8
sources.note: Mixed-precision MXFP4-Q8 MLX quantization for GLM-5; lists ~405GB footprint

sources.note: Community MLX run reporting ~15.4 tok/s and ~419GB memory on a single 512GB M3 Ultra

sources.title: Unsloth — Kimi K2 Thinking how to run locally (GGUF sizes; 1.8-bit min ~247GB combined for usable speed)
sources.note: Q4 Thinking GGUF is hundreds of GB; not a 128GB Mac Studio workload

sources.title: Hugging Face — unsloth/Kimi-K2-Thinking-GGUF (Q4_K_M and other quants)
sources.note: Shard-level public sizes for Unsloth Q4 and smaller dynamic quants

sources.title: Hugging Face — mlx-community (popular MLX conversions)
sources.note: Qwen3.5, GLM-4.7 Flash, gpt-oss-20B MXFP4, etc. — some rows use HF file-size hints for RAM gates

localModelProfiles.label: Phi-4 14B · Q4
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: 14B dense model; fits in 16GB configs. Scaled from Phi-4 MLX benchmarks (~20 tok/s M1 Pro → ~56 tok/s M1 Ultra); M4-tier extrapolated by memory bandwidth ratio. M5-tier scaled from M4 using memory-bandwidth ratio (M5: 153/120 GB/s = ×1.275; M5 Pro: 307/273 = ×1.12; M5 Max 32c: 460/410 = ×1.12; M5 Max 40c: 614/546 = ×1.12).

usefulness.note: Microsoft Phi-4: punches well above its weight on coding and STEM reasoning. Remarkably capable for its size; strong HumanEval scores.

localModelProfiles.label: Gemma 3 27B · Q4
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: M3 Ultra: 33 tok/s (LM Studio MLX, published benchmark). M4 Max extrapolated from bandwidth; M4 Pro 24GB is tight — needs ≥24GB config. M5 base scaled from M4 Pro 12c by bandwidth ratio (153/273); M5 Pro/Max scaled from M4 equivalents.

usefulness.note: Google Gemma 3 27B: strong multilingual and instruction-following. Good all-rounder; popular for low-memory frontier-quality inference.

localModelProfiles.label: Qwen3 30B-A3B · Q4 (MoE)
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: MoE: only 3.3B params active per token. M4 Max ~70-100 tok/s (HN community report); M4 Pro 16c ~89 tok/s TG (oMLX benchmark). Very fast due to sparse activation. M5 base scaled from M4 Pro 12c by bandwidth ratio; M5 Pro/Max scaled from M4 equivalents.

usefulness.note: Qwen3 30B-A3B: MoE efficiency delivers frontier-quality output at near-small-model speeds. 91.0 ArenaHard score; strong coding and reasoning. Exceptional value.

localModelProfiles.label: Qwen3.5 27B (Claude 4.6 Opus dist.) · Q4
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: Hugging Face `mlx-community/Qwen3.5-27B-Claude-4.6-Opus-Distilled-MLX-4bit`. Throughput profile aligned to Gemma 3 27B class (same ~17GB 4bit band as this site’s Gemma 3 row) — not an independent lab benchmark run.

usefulness.note: Distilled toward Claude Opus 4.6 behaviour; strong for coding and instruction following. Popular on the MLX hub.

localModelProfiles.label: SuperGemma4 26B (Jiunsong) · Q4
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: Hugging Face `Jiunsong/supergemma4-26b-uncensored-mlx-4bit-v2`. 26B ≈1GB lighter than 27B in RAM gate; tok/s nudged vs Gemma 3 27B — illustrative.

usefulness.note: Gemma-4 family uncensored MLX build; use case–dependent quality. High HF traction.

localModelProfiles.label: Qwen3.5 9B · Q4
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: Hugging Face `mlx-community/Qwen3.5-9B-MLX-4bit` (multimodal repo; text tok/s here ignores vision tower overhead). Slightly higher tok/s than Qwen 2.5 14B in this model’s weight class on Apple Silicon in practice — scaled from 14B row.

usefulness.note: Compact Qwen3.5; good for fast local chat on 16–24GB machines.

localModelProfiles.label: GLM-4.7 Flash · Q4
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: Hugging Face `mlx-community/GLM-4.7-Flash-4bit` — HF lists ~16.9GB. MoE-lite Z.ai Flash; tok/s nudged below Qwen3 30B-A3B row (smaller on-disk but similar tier).

usefulness.note: Z.ai GLM-4.7 Flash: low-latency agentic stack; strong zh/en. Popular MLX drop.

localModelProfiles.label: OpenAI gpt-oss-20B · MXFP4 (MLX)
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: Hugging Face `mlx-community/gpt-oss-20b-MXFP4-Q8` (repo name; ~12.1GB MLX file, 21B params). MXFP4 is not a separate Q4/Q8 row here — single profile. Scaled from 32B-class DeepSeek R1 row toward faster 20B — validate with mlx_lm on your box.

usefulness.note: Open-weights gpt-oss-20B with hybrid reasoning channels; one of the most downloaded MLX text repos.

localModelProfiles.label: Llama 3.1 8B · Q4
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: M4 Max ~90–100 tok/s (InsiderLLM MLX Q4). M3 Ultra scaled ~×1.47 vs Max by memory bandwidth. M5 tiers scaled from M4 equivalents by memory bandwidth ratio.

usefulness.note: Solid fast model for simple tasks; outclassed on reasoning by newer 14B+ models. Good for high-throughput chatbots.

localModelProfiles.label: Qwen 2.5 14B · Q4
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: Studio M4 Max ~55–65 tok/s for Qwen3 14B class (InsiderLLM); profile uses same ballpark for Qwen 2.5 14B. M5 tiers scaled from M4 equivalents by memory bandwidth ratio.

usefulness.note: Strong generalist at 14B. Competitive coding and instruction following; good balance of speed and quality.

localModelProfiles.label: DeepSeek R1 Distill Qwen 32B · Q4
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: Studio M4 Max ~30–40 tok/s for Qwen3 32B (InsiderLLM); used as proxy for 32B-class models. M5 tiers scaled from M4 equivalents by memory bandwidth ratio.

usefulness.note: R1 reasoning distillation makes this a strong everyday reasoning model. High Arena scores vs cost; popular community choice.

localModelProfiles.label: Llama 3.3 70B · Q4
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: Studio M4 Max ~15–18 tok/s; M3 Ultra ~25–30 tok/s for Llama 3.3 70B (InsiderLLM MLX Q4). M5 Pro needs 64GB config; M5 Max 32c up to 64GB (fine); M5 Max 40c up to 128GB.

usefulness.note: Meta's best open 70B. Broad general quality; a go-to when 32B falls short but frontier models are too slow.

localModelProfiles.label: Qwen3 235B · Q4
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: Large dense model; needs ~122GB unified RAM for Q4. Only M5 Max 40c (128GB config) can load this — tight headroom. Tok/s are order-of-magnitude (bandwidth-bound); clusters often used in practice.

usefulness.note: Top-tier open model. Near-frontier reasoning and coding; viable on M3 Ultra 256GB or cluster. Strong price-to-quality at the local frontier.

localModelProfiles.label: Llama 3.1 405B · Q4
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: ~200GB+ weights in Q4; realistically 256GB unified (M3 Ultra BTO). No single MacBook Pro config can host this. Throughput is illustrative — often run sharded across machines (e.g. exo).

usefulness.note: Meta's flagship open model. Strong across the board but eclipsed by MoE models at similar memory cost; mostly of interest for compliance/on-prem scenarios.

localModelProfiles.label: DeepSeek V3 · Q4 (MoE)
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: 671B total MoE; only part of weights active per token. Published cluster runs (e.g. 8× Mac minis) are aggregate tok/s — single-Studio numbers are rough guesses. No MacBook Pro config can host this model.

usefulness.note: Top-ranked open model on Chatbot Arena. Best-in-class coding and reasoning at time of release; requires a cluster or M3 Ultra 256GB for practical tok/s.

localModelProfiles.label: GLM-5 · Q4 (MLX 4-bit)
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: GLM-5 is a 744B total / 40B active MoE. MLX 4-bit card lists ~419GB footprint; community single-node M3 Ultra 512GB runs report ~15.4 tok/s. Treat as 512GB-class only on Apple Silicon.

usefulness.note: Z.ai GLM-5 became the leading open-weights model for coding and long-horizon agentic work in early 2026. Excellent frontier quality, but practical only on 512GB-class Apple Silicon.

localModelProfiles.label: Kimi K2 Thinking · Q4
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: Unsloth / community Q4_GGUF (e.g. Q4_K_M) is ~580–600GB on disk — a Mac Studio M4 Max 128GB cannot load this checkpoint. Native INT4 (QAT) is still data-center class in practice. M3 Ultra numbers are order-of-magnitude per-node in a 4× Mac Studio (M3 Ultra) exo/MLX cluster reporting ~25–28 tok/s aggregate (e.g. Virge, Jeff Geerling, Creative Strategies — not a lab single-node M4 Max bench). Simplismart’s ~173 tok/s is 8× NVIDIA B200, not Apple Silicon. For smaller Unsloth quants (e.g. UD-TQ1_0 ≈230GB) Unsloth cites ~1–2 tok/s with 256GB RAM and heavy offload, or 5+ tok/s with ≥247GB combined — not modeled on this Q4 row.

usefulness.note: Moonshot AI frontier reasoning model. Exceptional coding and long-context reasoning; requires high-memory configs. Emerging community favourite.

localModelProfiles.label: Llama 4 Scout · Q4 (MoE)
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: 109B total MoE, 17B active per token. Requires ~61GB Q4 — needs M4/M5 Max 128GB config (61+6GB OS overhead exceeds 64GB). Throughput estimated from 17B active-param bandwidth; 10M token context is impractical locally.

usefulness.note: Meta Llama 4 Scout: excellent long-context and multimodal capability. MoE efficiency helps, but still needs high-memory configs for local use.

localModelProfiles.label: Kimi K2.5 · Q4 (MoE)
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: 1T total MoE, 32B active. Q4 checkpoint ~620GB. Community reports show ~23–24 tok/s on 2× M3 Ultra 512GB via Exo/RDMA; per-node figures here are set to match that cluster band under the N^β speedup curve. Treat as community-derived, not standardized lab benchmark.

usefulness.note: Moonshot Kimi K2.5: 1T-param multimodal MoE released Feb 2026. Top-tier across benchmarks. Cluster-only at Q4 on Apple Silicon; best current evidence is community Exo/RDMA runs.

localModelProfiles.label: Phi-4 14B · Q8
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: Q8 row derived from Q4 throughput using memory-bandwidth scaling (~0.5× Q4 tok/s for dense models). Near-lossless quality, lower throughput.

usefulness.note: Q8 — near-lossless quality; speed estimated from Q4 by bandwidth ratio. Best for quality-sensitive coding and STEM tasks.

localModelProfiles.label: Gemma 3 27B · Q8
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: Q8 row derived from Q4 throughput using memory-bandwidth scaling (~0.5× Q4 tok/s). 34GB req exceeds M5 base max (32GB); needs M5 Pro 48GB config.

usefulness.note: Q8 — quality-focused Gemma 27B mode; throughput estimated from Q4.

localModelProfiles.label: Qwen3 30B-A3B · Q8 (MoE)
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: Q8 row derived from Q4 throughput using ~0.5× scaling; MoE remains relatively fast at Q8.

usefulness.note: Q8 — near-lossless quality while keeping strong MoE speed characteristics; throughput estimated from Q4.

localModelProfiles.label: Llama 3.1 8B · Q8
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: Q8 informed by published Apple Silicon results (M4 base ~11 tok/s, M4 Max ~42–55 tok/s) plus bandwidth scaling for remaining tiers.

usefulness.note: Q8 — higher fidelity than Q4 for an 8B baseline model; speed falls roughly in half.

localModelProfiles.label: Qwen 2.5 14B · Q8
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: Q8 anchored on published 14B Q8 samples (M4 Max ~29 tok/s) and scaled by memory bandwidth for other tiers.

usefulness.note: Q8 — higher quality 14B generalist profile; throughput estimated from Q4 with benchmark anchors.

localModelProfiles.label: DeepSeek R1 Distill Qwen 32B · Q8
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: Q8 row derived from Q4 throughput (~0.5×). Q8 32B is practical only on high-memory tiers.

usefulness.note: Q8 — quality-first 32B reasoning profile; throughput estimated from Q4.

localModelProfiles.label: Llama 3.3 70B · Q8
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: Q8 row derived from Q4 throughput (~0.5×) with M4 Max benchmark anchors around ~9 tok/s.

usefulness.note: Q8 — quality-focused 70B mode. High RAM requirement limits practical configs.

localModelProfiles.label: Qwen3 235B · Q8
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: Q8 row derived from Q4 throughput. Effective single-machine fit is extremely limited due to RAM requirements.

usefulness.note: Q8 — near-lossless frontier-class profile; practical mostly on clusters.

localModelProfiles.label: Llama 3.1 405B · Q8
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: Q8 row derived from Q4 throughput. Single-machine Apple configs cannot host this profile in practice.

usefulness.note: Q8 — research/reference profile only; cluster-scale deployment required.

localModelProfiles.label: DeepSeek V3 · Q8 (MoE)
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: Q8 row derived from Q4 throughput. High total weight footprint generally implies cluster usage.

usefulness.note: Q8 — highest-fidelity profile for DeepSeek V3 class; cluster-oriented.

localModelProfiles.label: GLM-5 · MXFP4-Q8 (MLX)
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: Custom MLX mixed-precision quant: experts in MXFP4, attention/embeddings/shared components in 8-bit. Model card lists ~405GB footprint. Public Apple Silicon tok/s data is sparse, so throughput is kept in the same conservative 15-16 tok/s M3 Ultra band as the 4-bit MLX runs.

usefulness.note: MXFP4-Q8 is the quality-focused MLX quant for GLM-5. Same frontier-class capability as the 4-bit row, with a mixed-precision weight layout that still needs 512GB-class Apple Silicon.

localModelProfiles.label: Kimi K2 Thinking · Q8
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: Q8 row: memory scaled ~2× Q4 (≈1.1TB-class); tok/s ≈ half the Q4 per-node cluster placeholders. Not a measured MLX row — no single-Mac config holds this footprint.

usefulness.note: Q8 — quality-focused Kimi profile; throughput estimated from Q4.

localModelProfiles.label: Llama 4 Scout · Q8 (MoE)
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: Q8 row derived from Q4 throughput with memory-bandwidth scaling.

usefulness.note: Q8 — quality-first Scout profile; high-memory hardware required.

localModelProfiles.label: Kimi K2.5 · Q8 (MoE)
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: Q8 row derived from Q4 community throughput (~0.5×). Requires multi-node 512GB-class clusters in practice.

usefulness.note: Q8 — community-derived cluster profile; cluster-only.

localModelProfiles.label: Llama 3.1 8B · F16
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: F16 row uses memory-bandwidth scaling (~0.25× Q4 tok/s), aligned with published M4 Max F16 samples (~25 tok/s).

usefulness.note: F16 — highest fidelity, lowest speed. Useful for exactness checks and eval parity.

localModelProfiles.label: Qwen 2.5 14B · F16
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: F16 row derived from Q4 throughput (~0.25×) for dense-model bandwidth behavior.

usefulness.note: F16 — quality-first 14B profile; significantly lower throughput.

localModelProfiles.label: Gemma 3 27B · F16
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: F16 row derived from Q4 throughput (~0.25×), with high RAM requirement (68GB) gating practical configs. Only M5 Max 40c (128GB) can host this.

usefulness.note: F16 — quality-optimized Gemma profile; practical mostly on 64GB+ tiers.

localModelProfiles.label: Qwen3 30B-A3B · F16 (MoE)
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: F16 row derived from Q4 throughput (~0.25×). MoE still helps relative to similarly sized dense models. 48GB req — M5 Pro needs 48GB config (tight); M5 Max 32c/40c fine.

usefulness.note: F16 — high-fidelity MoE profile with strong quality/speed trade-off on high-memory configs.

localModelProfiles.label: DeepSeek R1 Distill Qwen 32B · F16
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: F16 row derived from Q4 throughput (~0.25×). 96GB req exceeds M5 Pro/Max 32c max (64GB); M5 Max 40c (128GB) handles it.

usefulness.note: F16 — highest-fidelity 32B reasoning profile; throughput is substantially reduced.

localModelProfiles.label: Llama 3.3 70B · F16
localModelProfiles.benchmarkNote: F16 row derived from Q4 throughput (~0.25×). 192GB req far exceeds any MacBook Pro config; feasible only on 256GB-class Ultra configurations.

usefulness.note: F16 — best-fidelity 70B local profile; limited to very high-memory hardware.
